description: Phase-2 frontier: gen_7x7_s1_00 - a procedurally generated, seed-locked, uniqueness-verified tier-3 puzzle (formal grader: search_nodes=4547), 4 toolsets meta-off, n=3, with contamination control.
motivation: Map the granularity benefit at the hard end of a formally-graded difficulty axis, and test G-CD-v1's prediction P3 (benefit grows with difficulty).
formal_results: cost mu+-sigma (n=3): none $2.08+-0.58 (2.3 calls); fine $3.21+-1.41 (82 calls); medium $2.69+-0.93 (45.7); coarse $2.07+-0.67 (29.3). coarse/none = 1.00x; ALL four CIs overlap at 1 sigma (within $1.14). $/call: none $2.04 vs fine $0.037 = 55x.
results_description: At tier-3 the strong tier-2 granularity separation collapses - all toolsets cost the same within noise - producing a V-shaped cost-ratio surface across tiers, the opposite of a monotonic benefit.
conclusions: Falsifies G-CD-v1's monotonic P3; supports the Goldilocks two-floor revision (benefit only at intermediate difficulty) and sharpens the P4 calls/cost decoupling (alpha << beta). Caveat: this single puzzle may be cost-cheap by sparse clues despite the tier-3 grade.
report: Full window-by-window detail for the Phase-2 frontier (gen_7x7_s1_00, windows W10-W18) and the Phase-2 final verdict (V-shape held at n=3, P3-prime PASS) is in the stream-level report (source: notes/2026-05-18..., section "Phase-2 final verdict"). The G-CD-v1 -> v2 theory revision this result triggered is the report on a-0011 (source: notes/2026-05-23_gcd_revision_goldilocks.md).
methodology: Phase-2 difficulty frontier on gen_7x7_s1_00, a procedurally generated tier-3 puzzle with seed-locked uniqueness verification (contamination control). 4 toolsets meta-off, n=3 per cell, Opus 4.7, max_turns=200, via harness/run_plan.py over multiple windows. Formal difficulty graded by core/generator.py (search_nodes); analysis and figures via harness/analyze_phase2.py.
